mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically positive at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which triggers immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and driving sustained positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days in backtests, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates short-term positive equity momentum during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually, or 5-day forward returns during industrial production uptrends are negative in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings erode real corporate profit margins and force central banks to tighten policy expectations, creating immediate downward pressure on equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes consistently coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the correlation coefficient exceeds +0.10 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 300,000 signal robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and generates positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above 300,000 fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the signal exhibits negative rank IC during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales demonstrate synchronized strength in durable goods and consumer spending, confirming aggregate demand resilience and triggering positive equity momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal in housing and retail data yields negative mean forward returns over 20 days, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary stance that compresses equity multiples, producing immediate negative short-term returns during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when both inflation and rates rise, or the mechanism fails to separate bull and bear market outcomes.
